page: OVHcloud b3-8 is a general-purpose virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs utilizing the AMD EPYC Milan processor family running at 2.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a local 50 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with the stress-ng single-core CPU test and Geekbench single-core score both placing in the top 25% of tested servers. However, multi-core performance is more limited, with Geekbench multi-core and Passmark CPU scores falling into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth for large blocks is average at 62.95 GB/sec for read operations and 45.99 GB/sec for write operations, though small-block cached read bandwidth is relatively weak.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and strong single-threaded capabilities, the b3-8 is suited for workloads that do not require massive parallelization. It performs well in text processing and basic single-threaded tasks but shows weaknesses in multi-threaded decompression, database operations, and static web serving. This makes it a viable option for lightweight application hosting, development environments, and basic general-purpose computing where dedicated CPU resources are preferred over shared-core alternatives.
description: OVHcloud b3-8 is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor and powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor. It delivers 2 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores at 2.3 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of RAM and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. While the instance demonstrates strong single-core performance in benchmarks, placing in the top 25% for single-threaded CPU and text processing tests, its multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities are more modest, landing in the lower percentiles. This resource profile offers a balanced, dedicated hardware allocation without hyperthreading, making it a reliable and cost-efficient option for lightweight workloads, development environments, and single-threaded applications that require predictable CPU performance.
og_description: OVHcloud b3-8 is a general-purpose KVM instance featuring 2 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and a 50 GB NVMe SSD, offering strong single-core performance for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud b3-8 is a general-purpose KVM server featuring 2 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A dedicated dual-core virtual server featuring AMD EPYC processors and NVMe storage for reliable, single-threaded general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 2 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs with 2.3 GHz clock speed
8.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for low-latency I/O
Strong single-core benchmark performance in the top 25%
Best suited for lightweight applications, development, and text processing
